risinfo.chaptertitle: 7 Non-Preventive Evaluation and Management Services in Outpatient Settings

sect1.title: CHAPTER 7. NON-PREVENTIVE EVALUATION AND MANAGEMENT SERVICES IN OUTPATIENT SETTINGS
sect1.para: Third-party payers will have specific policies for coverage and payment.

listitem.para: Some carriers pay practices for extended hours because they recognize the cost benefit realized from decreased urgent care and ED visits.
Communicate with individual payers to understand their definition or interpretation of the service and their coverage and payment policies.
As part of this negotiation and education process, it is important to demonstrate the cost savings recognized by the payer for these adjunct services.

If appropriate, more than one adjunct code may be reported on the same day of service (eg, 99058 and 99051 for services provided on an emergency basis during regularly scheduled evening or weekend hours).

sect1.para: Many payers will pay only for the use of a single special services code per encounter and may manually review, question, or deny payment for a claim with multiple after-hours codes.

Follow individual payer policy when reporting after-hours codes in conjunction with a service provided via telemedicine. Codes 99050 and 99051 are neither preceded by a telemedicine symbol (★ or 4) nor included in Appendix P or T of the American Medical Association CPT manual. Therefore, these codes are not reported with telemedicine services.

sect2.title: Services Provided When the Office Is Normally Closed
sect2.para: 99050 Service(s) provided in office at times other than regularly scheduled office hours, or days when the office is normally closed (eg, holidays, Saturday, Sunday), in addition to basic service

listitem.para: Office hours must be posted. CPT does not define a holiday or posted office hours. Although most commonly applied to evening, weekend, or holiday hours, 99050 could be applied to services provided at the patient request on a weekday if the office is typically closed on that day.
Code 99050 is not reported when a physician or other QHP is behind schedule and sees patients after posted office hours.
The service must be requested by the patient, and the physician or other QHP must agree to see the patient.
Documentation must indicate the time and date of the encounter and the request to be seen outside of normal posted hours.

sect4.title: Services Provided During Regularly Scheduled Evening, Weekend, or Holiday Hours
sect4.para: 99051 Service(s) provided in the office during regularly scheduled evening, weekend, or holiday office hours, in addition to basic service

listitem.para: Regularly scheduled office hours must be posted.
Documentation must include the time and date of the encounter.

Evenings and holidays are not defined by CPT, but holidays can generally refer to national and/or state holidays, and evenings are generally 6:00 pm and later. Check with payers for coverage and/or ability to bill patients these charges under the health plan contract.

sect4.para: Example ▶ A pediatric clinic offers urgent care appointments 3 evenings a week with staffing by QHPs (eg, NPs, physician associates) working under general supervision. A patient who has been seen at the clinic by Dr A within the past year is seen in the evening clinic by an NP who documents a clinically indicated history and examination with low-level MDM with diagnosis of URI.

table.title: Services Provided During Regularly Scheduled Evening, Weekend, or Holiday Hours

row.entry: ICD-10-CM
CPT
J06.9 (acute URI, unspecified)
99213 (established office E/M)99051 (service provided in office during regularly scheduled weekend hours)

listitem.para: indicates a new code; ▲, revised; #, re-sequenced; ★, add-on; ✚, audiovisual technology; and 4, synchronous interactive audio.

sect4.title: Services Provided on an Emergency Basis in the Office
sect4.para: 99058 Service(s) provided on an emergency basis in the office, which disrupts other scheduled office services, in addition to basic service

listitem.para: Report when an office patient's condition, in the clinical judgment of the physician, warrants the physician's interrupting care of another patient to deal with the emergency.
Code 99058 may not be reported when patients are simply fit into the schedule or for walk-ins.
Document that the patient was seen immediately and the reason for the emergent care.

sect4.para: Example ▶ A pediatric practice offers scheduled appointments on Saturday mornings from 8:00 am to noon. A child is scheduled for evaluation of increased symptoms of moderate persistent asthma on a Saturday at 9:00 am. The child arrives 30 minutes before the scheduled time with severe exacerbation and is immediately taken to an examination room by the triage nurse, and the physician disrupts his schedule to see the child urgently.

table.title: Services Provided on an Emergency Basis in the Office

row.entry: ICD-10-CM
CPT
J45.42 (moderate persistent asthma with status asthmaticus)

99202–99215 (new or established office E/M)99058 (service provided on an emergency basis in office, disrupting other scheduled services)99051 (service provided in office during regularly scheduled weekend hours)

sect4.title: Out-of-Office Service (99056–99060)
sect4.para: Codes 99056–99060 are used to report services provided after hours or on an emergency basis and are an adjunct to the basic E/M service provided.

Third-party payers have specific policies for coverage and payment. Communicate with individual payers to understand their definition or interpretation of the service and their coverage and payment policies. As part of this negotiation and education process, it is important to demonstrate the cost savings recognized by the payer for these adjunct services. After-hour service codes are used by physicians or other QHPs (under their state scope of practice when billing with their own NPI) to identify the services that are adjunct to the basic services rendered. These codes

listitem.para: Describe the special circumstances under which a basic procedure is performed
Are reported only in addition to an associated basic service (eg, home visit)
Are reported without a modifier appended to the basic service because they only further describe the services provided

sect4.para: 99056 Service(s) typically provided in the office, provided out of the office at request of patient, in addition to basic service Documentation should include the patient's request to be seen outside the office.

99060 Service(s) provided on an emergency basis, out of the office, which disrupts other scheduled office services, in addition to basic service Documentation should indicate that the physician was called away during scheduled office hours to attend to a patient in another location (eg, a nursing facility).

Example ▶ A physician is seeing patients in the office when a parent calls and asks the physician to provide a house call for her child who would otherwise require specialized transport. The child has a fever and appears quite ill. The physician agrees and, later that day, arrives at the patient's home to find that the patient is more withdrawn than typical. The physician spends a total of 40 minutes providing the home visit, which includes examining the child, diagnosing streptococcal sore throat based on clinical signs and symptoms, and prescribing an antibiotic.

sect4.title: | ICD-10-CM | CPT |

row.entry: J02.0 (streptococcal pharyngitis) Assign codes for conditions that limit mobility.

99349 (established home or residence E/M requiring moderate MDM or total time of 40 minutes or more) 99056 (service[s] typically provided in the office, provided out of the office at request of patient, in addition to basic service)
J02.0 (streptococcal pharyngitis) Assign codes for conditions that limit mobility.

99349 (established home or residence E/M requiring moderate MDM or total time of 40 minutes or more) 99056 (service[s] typically provided in the office, provided out of the office at request of patient, in addition to basic service)

sect4.para: Teaching Point: Payers may or may not allow payment for 99056 when services are rendered in a patient's home or residence or when a physician meets a patient in the ED. But it is appropriate to report 99056 when a service is provided at an alternative location at the request from the patient and/or caregiver. It is inappropriate to report 99056 when a physician instructs a patient to meet at a location other than the office. The POS on the claim should reflect where the encounter took place (eg, 12, patient's home).
To test your knowledge of the information presented in this chapter, complete the quiz found at the end of it, after the continuums and resources. Answers to each quiz are found in Appendix II.

listitem.para: indicates a new code; ▲, revised; #, re-sequenced; ✚, add-on; ✱, modifier 51 exempt; ⊕, audiovisual technology; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio.

sect2.title: Asthma, Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder, and Otitis Media: Coding Continuums

sect4.title: Continuum Model for Asthma
sect4.para: Code selection atany level above 99211 may be based on the level of MDM or the total time spent on the DOS by the reporting physician or other QHP. The extent of history and examination documented does not affect code selection.

row.entry: CPT Code and Vignette
Medical Decision-Making (2 of 3 Elements Required)
99211Nurse visit for a well 10-year-old established patient

Time and MDM do not apply. Must indicate continuation of physician's plan of care, medical necessity, assessment, and/or education provided. Chief complaint: Review asthma care plan as ordered at last visit with doctor. Current medications documented, peak expiratory flow measured, education topics reviewed, and questions answered
No. and Complexity of Problems Addressed
Amount and/or Complexity of Data Reviewed and Analyzed
Risk of Complications and/or Morbidity or Mortality of Patient Management
99212 (≥10 minutes)An 8-year-old with stable asthma presents with a single patch of poison ivy rash on her forearm.
SF: 1 self-limited or minor problem
SF: None
SF: Minimal risk of morbidity resulting from treatment
99213 (≥20 minutes)A 7-year-old with persistent asthma who is using a metered dose steroid inhaler with β-agonist as needed returns for follow-up.
Low: 1 stable chronic illness
Limited: Assessment requiring an independent historian(s)—Mother provides history of asthma symptoms and medication adherence since most recent visit.
Moderate: Prescription drug management
99214 (≥30 minutes)An 8-year-old with asthma is examined because of an acute exacerbation of the disease.
Moderate: ≥1 chronic illnesses with exacerbation, progression, or side effects of treatment

Limited: Assessment requiring an independent historian(s)—Mother provides history. Note: Spirometry and pulse oximetry provided are separately reported and not counted toward data reviewed and analyzed.
Moderate: Prescription drug management
CPT Code and Vignette
Medical Decision-Making (2 of 3 Elements Required)
CPT Code With Total Physician Time and Vignette
No. and Complexity of Problems Addressed
Amount and/or Complexity of Data Reviewed and Analyzed
Risk of Complications and/or Morbidity or Mortality of Patient Management

99215 (≥40 minutes)An 8-year-old with unstable asthma presents with severe exacerbation of the disease and poor response to inhaler treatments at home. Oral steroids are administered with significant improvement. Hospitalization is discussed, but a decision is made for continued treatment at home with close monitoring.
High: 1 chronic condition with severe exacerbation
Limited: Assessment requiring an independent historian(s)
High: Decision regarding hospitalization

listitem.para: indicates a new code; ▲, revised; #, re-sequenced; ✚, add-on; ✱, audiovisual technology; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio.

para.emphasis: Code selection atany level above 99211 may be based on the level of MDM or the total time spent by the physician or other QHP on the date of the encounter.

row.entry: CPT Code With Total Physician Time and Vignette
No. and Complexity of Problems Addressed
Amount and/or Complexity of Data Reviewed and Analyzed
Risk of Complications and/or Morbidity or Mortality of Patient Management
99211Nurse visit to check growth or blood pressure before renewing prescription for psychoactive drugs

Time and MDM do not apply. Must indicate continuation of physician's plan of care, medical necessity, assessment, and/or education provided. Chief complaint: Check growth or blood pressure as ordered at last visit with doctor. Documentation: Height, weight, and blood pressure. Existing medications and desired/undesired effects. Assessment: Doing well. Obtained physician approval for prescription refill. Advised to keep appointment with physician in 1 month.
99212 (≥10 minutes)4-year-old whose parents are concerned about ADHD symptoms (ADHD is not diagnosed; parents are reassured.)
Minimal: 1 self-limited problem
Limited: Assessment requiring an independent historian
Minimal: Parent education
99213 (≥20 minutes)Initial follow-up after initiation of medication, patient responding well
Low: 1 stable chronic illness
Limited: Assessment requiring an independent historian
Moderate: Prescription drug management
99214 (≥30 minutes)Follow-upfor recent weight loss inpatient with established ADHD otherwise stable when taking stimulant medication
Moderate: 1 chronic illness with side effects of treatment
Limited: Assessment requiring an independent historian
Moderate: Prescription drug management

99215 (≥40 minutes)Initial evaluation of a patient with ADHD and new onset of suicidal ideation. The patient and mother decide on no hospitalization because of cost. Tip: Add 99417 if time on the DOS is ≥55 minutes. Add 99058 if service(s) are provided on an emergency basis in the office, which disrupts other scheduled office services.
High: 1 acute or chronic illness or injury that poses a threat to life or bodily function
Moderate: Assessment requiring an independent historian; discussion with behavioral health specialist; psychiatric testing
High: Decision regarding hospitalization

para.emphasis: Code selection atany level above 99211 may be based on the level of MDM or the total time spent by the physician or other QHP on the date of the encounter. (Code 99211 is not included because of lack of indication for follow-up by clinical staff.)

row.entry: CPT Code With Total Physician Time and Vignette
No. and Complexity of Problems Addressed
Amount and/or Complexity of Data Reviewed and Analyzed
Risk of Complications and/or Morbidity or Mortality of Patient Management
99212 (≥10 minutes)Follow-upfor otitis media, uncomplicated
Minimal: Follow-upfor otitis media, evaluation of effusion and hearing
Limited: Assessment requiring an independent historian
Minimal: Risk associated with diagnostic testing and treatment
99213 (≥20 minutes)2-year-old presents with tugging ather right ear. Afebrile. Mild otitis media.
Low: 1 acute, uncomplicated illness or injury
Limited: Assessment requiring an independent historian
Moderate: Prescription drug management, delayed prescribing
99214 (≥30 minutes)Infant presents with fever, lethargy, and cough and suspected third episode of otitis media within 3 months.
Moderate: 1 acute illness with systemic symptoms
Limited: Assessment requiring an independent historian
Moderate: Prescription drug management
99215 (≥40 minutes)6-year-old presents with fever, severe left ear pain, and protrusion consistent with mastoiditis.
High: 1 acute illness that poses a threat to life or bodily function
Moderate: Assessment requiring an independent historian
High: Decision about hospitalization

blockquote.para: This chapter focused on outpatient services that are reported according to either MDM or the total time by a physician or QHP on the date of the encounter. Readers of this chapter should generally understand outpatient code categories and code selection. Following are takeaways from this chapter:

listitem.para: Codes for outpatient E/M services are selected according to the site of service as directed by CPT guidelines.

The key determinant for reporting G2211 is the intent for a long-term physician-patient relationship either as a focal point for all of a patient's health care needs or for management of a single serious or complex condition.
To be considered a new patient, the patient must not have received a professional service from a physician or QHP of the same exact specialty and same group practice within the past 3 years.
Office E/M services provided solely by clinical staff may be reported with 99211. Payer policy may limit reporting to services that meet an incident-to policy.

For all the outpatient visits discussed in this chapter except 99211, the level of service is determined by either the level of MDM alone or by the total amount of time spent by a physician or other QHP on the DOS directed to care of the patient.
Prolonged service may be reported when the time requirements are met.
Critical care provided in outpatient settings is reported with time-based codes 99291 and 99292.

Use 99050–99060 in addition to the code for services provided when special circumstances apply (eg, a service provided after the practice's normal business hours to allow care in the practice as opposed to an urgent care clinic or ED).

para.emphasis: ● indicates a new code; ▲, revised; #, re-sequenced; ✚, add-on; ✱, audiovisual technology; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio.

sect4.title: AAP Coding Assistance and Education
sect4.para: AAP Initial History Questionnaire documentation form for office or outpatient visits (www.aap.org/Initial-History-Questionnaire-Documentation-Form-Pack-of-50) Coding for Visit Complexity for Office/Other Outpatient Services (https://downloads.aap.org/AAP/PDF/AAP%20Factsheet%20Complexity%20Add%20On%20G2211.pdf)
Pediatric Evaluation and Management: Coding Quick Reference Card 2026 (www.aap.org/Pediatric-Evaluation-and-Management-Coding-Quick-Reference-Card-2026)

sect4.title: AAP News™
sect4.para: "Understanding updated E/M coding guidelines can prevent underbilling, loss of revenue," August 1, 2024 (https://publications.aap.org/aapnews/news/29460)

sect4.title: AAP Pediatric Coding Newsletter
sect4.para: "Answers to Questions About Evaluation and Management Code Selection in 2023," January 2023 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book225 document002) "Compliance Corner: Incident to Services," November 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book247 document003)
E/M 2021 and 2023 coding resources (https://publications.aap.org/codingnews/info-parent/27376/Coding-Resources)
"AAP Position on Medicare Consultation Policy"
"Critical Care Reporting in Outpatient Settings" "Evaluation and Management Service Coding in Nursing and Residential Treatment Facilities"

"Incident-to Services by Nonphysician Professionals and Clinical Staff: Medicare Requirements" ● indicates a new code; ▲, revised; †, re-sequenced; ✚, add-on; ✱, audiovisual technology; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio.

para.emphasis: Which statement is true for reporting G2211 to indicate an office E/M (99202–99205, 99211–99215) was part of longitudinal care?

listitem.para: Do not report G2211 when reporting an office E/M service to address a problem (eg, 99213 25) and an influenza vaccine administration (eg, 90460) on the same date.
G2211 is reported in addition to an office E/M code only when no other codes are reported on the same date.
Do not report G2211 in addition to code 99211.
G2211 is reported in addition to an office E/M code appended with modifier 25 only when reporting certain preventive services (eg, 90460–90461, immunization administration) on the same date.

para.emphasis: True or false? A service provided by clinical staff under the supervision of a physician or QHP who is immediately available via real-time audio-video telecommunications technology may be reported with 99211.

listitem.para: True
False

para.emphasis: Which is true of the total time included in an office E/M code descriptor?

listitem.para: The time included in the code descriptor is the maximum time reported with the code.
The time included in the code descriptor must bemet or exceeded.
The time included in the code descriptor includes only a physician's or QHP's face-to-face time with the patient and/or caregiver.
Total time includes all time in a 24-hour period.

para.emphasis: Using Table 7-1 to guide code selection, which code is supported by documentation of a new patient office visit including 12 minutes, total time and straightforward MDM?

listitem.para: The visit cannot be reported because the code descriptor for 99202 requires at least 15 minutes of total time.
The information in the question is insufficient for code selection.

para.emphasis: What information is necessary to support selection of prolonged clinical staff service code 99415?

listitem.para: The amount of clinical staff time that is beyond the total time spent by the reporting physician or QHP
Only the total time spent by the reporting physician or QHP
The office E/M code selected by the physician or QHP, based on MDM or total time, and the total face-to-face clinical staff time
Only the total clinical staff time including both face-to-face and non-face-to-face time
